_note: Manual in-game crafting difficulty-color observations, used to reverse-engineer recipe trivials the wiki is missing (it shows '?'). Difficulty colors easiest->hardest: green (= trivial, no more skill-ups), lightblue, darkblue, white (~50% success), yellow, orange, red. A recipe seen GREEN at skill S has trivial <= S; any other color means trivial > S. Recording the same recipe at several skill levels pins the exact trivial (it turns green at its trivial) and calibrates each colour's skill-gap. Edit this file as you hit new skill milestones; the site merges it over the wiki recipe data.

_model.note: Trivials land on MULTIPLES OF 5 (in-game breakpoints are every 5 levels), and each colour band is 5 skill points wide. This single model reproduces the skill-48, 50, 55, 75 AND 81 readings (5 milestones) with 0 mismatches. Two boundary-shade misreads were corrected against Zak's eyes: at 75 the bronze components + silver looked 'green' but are LIGHT-BLUE (76-80); at 55 silver looked 'red' but is ORANGE (= 80). Green/teal and orange/red are easy to confuse in screenshots. Red items remain a lower bound until they shift colour.

Blacksmithing.note: All copper recipes not listed here were green/trivial at skill 48. Enchanted variants matched their base item's colour.
greenTrivialAtOrBelow: (and every other copper recipe not listed above)

Blacksmithing.note: +2 skill from the level-48 reading. Confirms the multiples-of-5 / 5-wide-band model: the level-48 lightblue recipes are now exactly green (trivial 50). Enchanted variants match their base item's colour.
greenTrivialAtOrBelow: (and every other copper recipe not listed above)

Blacksmithing.note: Copper War Lance turned GREEN at exactly 55 (confirms trivial 55). Copper weapons read yellow (= 75). Bronze tier + jagged stones read orange (= 80). Silver was recorded RED here, but later readings prove it's 80 (orange) — this was an orange/red boundary misread.
orange: Silver Chain Links (recorded red; is orange = 80)

Blacksmithing.note: Big jump from 55. Correction: an initial read called the bronze components + jagged stones + silver 'green', but Zak confirmed Bronze Bracket/Chain Links and Silver Chain Links are LIGHT-BLUE here (76-80, matching orange@55). Green/teal is hard to tell apart in screenshots. Enchanted variants match their base.

Fifth reading, fits the model exactly with the corrected 75 shades. Silver Plate is now GREEN (confirms Silver = 80, matching the wiki). Reveals the upper bronze tier. 'Enchanted Bronze Chain Links' appeared RED here, which conflicts with its base (Bronze Chain Links = 80) — FLAGGED for a recheck (could be a distinct harder recipe, or a red/orange misread).
green: (bronze components, jagged stones and all copper — every recipe <= 80)
red: Enchanted Bronze Chain Links (FLAGGED - conflicts with base 80)

trivialEstimates._method: Derived from the colour at skill 48, 50, 55, 75 AND 81 via the multiples-of-5 / 5-wide-band model, 0 mismatches. Copper line confirmed (50-75). Silver = 80 (Silver Plate green at 81; matches the wiki — the earlier 85 was a 55 orange/red misread). Bronze tier maps cleanly 80 -> 110+. Red items (Bronze Great Scythe / Longsword / Spear / Trident / Chain Boots / Plate Boots) are a lower bound of ~110 until they shift colour. Enchanted variants share their base trivial EXCEPT 'Enchanted Bronze Chain Links', which read red at 81 (conflicts with base 80) and is left out pending a recheck.
